title: Assessment of prenatal exposure to arsenic in Tenerife Island.
ehr_evidence: The study used questionnaires and meconium samples, not electronic health records.
summary: A transversal descriptive study assessing prenatal arsenic exposure via meconium analysis in 96 mother-child pairs in Tenerife, Spain, and its association with maternal diet and lifestyle.
key_findings: Arsenic was detected in 38.5% of meconium samples.
Frequent intake of vegetables was associated with detectable meconium arsenic levels.
No association was found between arsenic exposure and neonatal effects.
